title: MCAD deficiency: hypoketotic hypoglycemia on fasting
vignette: An 8-year-old boy is brought to the emergency department due to vomiting and lethargy. The patient had been on an overnight hiking trip with his family. During the trip, the family lost their food pack while canoeing and had to hike back to their car. The child became weak and was carried for the last mile. None of the family has eaten for approximately 24 hours. On examination, the patient appears listless. Mild hepatomegaly is noted. Laboratory results are as follows: glucose 22 mg/dL, acetoacetate not detected, aspartate aminotransferase 47 U/L, alanine aminotransferase 53 U/L. The patient begins seizing shortly after arriving at the emergency department. Which of the following enzymes is most likely deficient in this patient?

clues.text: None of the family has eaten for approximately 24 hours
clues.note: A prolonged fast is the metabolic stress that unmasks fatty acid oxidation defects. After glycogen is depleted, the body must oxidize fatty acids and make ketones - which fails in MCAD deficiency.

Hepatomegaly from lipid accumulation occurs because fatty acids cannot be oxidized and back up in the liver. Together with hypoketotic hypoglycemia, this fits medium-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase (MCAD) deficiency.

Severe hypoglycemia (glucose 22 / normal 70-100) after fasting. Without fatty acid oxidation, tissues can't spare glucose and the liver can't sustain gluconeogenesis, causing profound hypoglycemia and seizures.

Absent ketones (acetoacetate not detected) during fasting hypoglycemia = HYPOKETOTIC hypoglycemia, the hallmark of a fatty acid oxidation defect. Normally fasting should raise ketones; their absence points to blocked beta-oxidation.

whatMatters: Fasting + severe hypoglycemia + ABSENT ketones (hypoketotic) + hepatomegaly in a child = a fatty acid beta-oxidation defect, classically MCAD deficiency. The deficient enzyme is (medium-chain) acyl-CoA dehydrogenase - answer C.
strategy: Pattern: fasting/illness -> hypoglycemia WITH inappropriately LOW ketones (hypoketotic) -> fatty acid oxidation defect (MCAD). Contrast: glycogen storage (von Gierke, G6Pase) gives fasting hypoglycemia but WITH lactic acidosis and often ketosis; ketotic hypoglycemia has ketones present. Absent ketones is the key that selects acyl-CoA dehydrogenase.

eliminations.label: acetyl-CoA carboxylase - fatty acid SYNTHESIS, wrong direction
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: ACC commits acetyl-CoA to malonyl-CoA for fatty acid SYNTHESIS. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: the problem here is failed fatty acid BREAKDOWN (beta-oxidation) during fasting, not synthesis; an ACC defect wouldn't cause hypoketotic hypoglycemia. ✅ Correct if: a disorder of lipogenesis were described.

eliminations.label: acid alpha-glucosidase - Pompe disease, no fasting hypoglycemia
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: lysosomal acid alpha-glucosidase; deficiency = Pompe disease (lysosomal glycogen storage). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: Pompe causes cardiomegaly/hypotonia, not fasting hypoketotic hypoglycemia. ✅ Correct if: an infant with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and muscle weakness.

eliminations.label: glucose 6-phosphatase - von Gierke, but with lactic acidosis and ketosis
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: G6Pase deficiency = von Gierke disease (glycogen storage type I). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: it causes fasting hypoglycemia but WITH lactic acidosis, hyperuricemia, hyperlipidemia, and usually ketosis - not the ABSENT ketones seen here. You chose this. ✅ Correct if: fasting hypoglycemia with high lactate, uric acid, and triglycerides plus hepatomegaly.

eliminations.label: glycogen phosphorylase - McArdle/Hers, different picture
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: glycogen phosphorylase deficiency (muscle = McArdle, liver = Hers). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: McArdle causes exercise intolerance/cramps (not hypoglycemia); hepatic Hers causes mild fasting hypoglycemia but with ketosis, not hypoketotic. ✅ Correct if: exercise-induced cramps with myoglobinuria (McArdle) or mild ketotic hypoglycemia (Hers).

reasoning: Prolonged fast (24 h) unmasks the defect after glycogen runs out
Severe hypoglycemia WITH absent ketones = hypoketotic hypoglycemia
Hypoketotic hypoglycemia + hepatomegaly = fatty acid beta-oxidation defect (MCAD)
Deficient enzyme = (medium-chain) acyl-CoA dehydrogenase -> answer C
mechanism: During fasting, after hepatic glycogen is depleted, the body relies on fatty acid beta-oxidation to generate acetyl-CoA for hepatic ketogenesis and to spare glucose. Medium-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase (MCAD) catalyzes the first step of each beta-oxidation cycle for medium-chain fatty acids; its deficiency blocks fat oxidation, so the liver cannot make ketones (hypoketotic) and cannot sustain energy/gluconeogenesis, producing severe fasting hypoglycemia, lethargy, vomiting, seizures, and hepatomegaly from accumulating fat. This contrasts with glycogen storage diseases (e.g., von Gierke/G6Pase: fasting hypoglycemia WITH lactic acidosis and ketosis) and with fatty acid synthesis defects. Treatment is avoidance of fasting and dextrose during illness.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Biochemistry > Fatty acid oxidation: MCAD deficiency = autosomal recessive, fasting hypoketotic hypoglycemia, vomiting/lethargy/seizures, hepatomegaly, elevated dicarboxylic acids; treat by avoiding fasting. Contrast with von Gierke (G6Pase: lactic acidosis) and Pompe (acid maltase).

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: The discriminating lab is ABSENT ketones with hypoglycemia (hypoketotic). That single feature selects a beta-oxidation defect (acyl-CoA dehydrogenase) over glycogen storage diseases.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on beta-oxidation, hypoketotic hypoglycemia, MCAD, acyl-CoA dehydrogenase, von Gierke/G6Pase, ketogenesis? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: You chose D (glucose 6-phosphatase / von Gierke). That also causes fasting hypoglycemia but WITH lactic acidosis and ketosis - here ketones are ABSENT, which points to fatty acid oxidation (MCAD).

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Anchor: fasting hypoglycemia + LOW/absent ketones = fatty acid oxidation defect (acyl-CoA dehydrogenase).

keywords.front: Fasting hypoglycemia + absent ketones (hypoketotic) + hepatomegaly in a child

keywords.back: First step of each fatty acid beta-oxidation cycle

keywords.front: Why no ketones in MCAD deficiency
keywords.back: Blocked beta-oxidation -> no acetyl-CoA for hepatic ketogenesis
WITH lactic acidosis, hyperuricemia, hyperlipidemia, ketosis
Avoid fasting; give dextrose (IV glucose) during illness

firstAid.detail: Biochem: glycogen depletion, gluconeogenesis, ketogenesis timeline.
firstAid.location: Biochem: glycogen depletion, gluconeogenesis, ketogenesis timeline.

firstAid.detail: Biochem: von Gierke (G6Pase), Pompe (acid maltase), McArdle/Hers (phosphorylase).
firstAid.location: Biochem: von Gierke (G6Pase), Pompe (acid maltase), McArdle/Hers (phosphorylase).

memoryHook: Fast + LOW glucose but NO ketones = fat-burning is broken (MCAD). 'Hypoketotic = can't burn fat' = acyl-CoA dehydrogenase.
